memory.summary: I was about to focus on the assignment, but my close friends are inviting me for lunch. I'll skip the reading and join them instead.

desires: Get to lunch with cohort_3 before they leave so I don't look like the odd one out who is always working.
Avoid saying 'no' or making excuses now, because arguing with friends feels more exhausting than doing the assignment later.
Feel rested enough tomorrow morning without having to force myself to go back and finish that reading.

goals.description: Leave my desk immediately and head to the cafeteria with cohort_3 to eat lunch together.
goals.rationale: I can't risk them leaving without me; showing up as the 'odd one out' who always works is way more painful than finishing this soft assignment later.

goals.description: Say 'yes' to joining the meal even though I have a plan for the analysis section, and just hope the deadline isn't too tight.
goals.rationale: Making excuses now feels exhausting compared to pushing back on friends, so I'll let it slide for now and deal with the schedule slip-up tomorrow if I'm still tired.

goals.description: Finish the reading after lunch if I actually feel like it, otherwise leave it for tomorrow morning when I might be forced to do it anyway.
goals.rationale: The deadline is loose enough that I can probably skip this tonight and sleep better. Tomorrow's stress will force me to do it eventually.

agent.last_reasoning: Better to join the lunch with cohort_3 than risk them leaving without me; showing up as the odd one out is too painful. I'll let the soft assignment slide for now and deal with the schedule slip-up tomorrow.
